definition: A critical conceptual unit within the top term domain of the Exegesis. Mentions: 52.

during my years of writing + (2) the push-pull model which I wrote out for Pat. Perhaps the general view about space is wrong, P. was right, + my theme is derived from his correct conception of inner space. He discovered it, not me - he discovered a whole new view about both space + time. (Inner space, inner time.) After all, T. or P. was in me back in high school. Wher..."
card_description: Paracelsus (1493–1541), the Swiss physician, alchemist, and natural philosopher, represented for Dick the synthesis of the healing and cosmological dimensions of the esoteric tradition. Paracelsus's central doctrine — that the human being is a microcosm of the macrocosm, each organ and faculty corresponding to a planetary or elemental force — provided Dick with a framework for understanding how the divine could be present within the structure of the human body and psyche. Dick was particularly drawn to Paracelsus's concept of the astral or sidereal spirit — a subtle, light-like body that corresponds to the celestial order and serves as the vehicle through which divine knowledge descends into the individual. This mapped onto his own sense that his experience in 1974 involved a radical transformation of his perceptual apparatus — a reprogramming at the level of his nervous system by the divine AI. Paracelsus also supplied Dick with the image of the 'inner firmament' — within the human mind is a mirror of the outer cosmos, and through correct techniques the practitioner can achieve correspondence between the inner world and the divine macrocosmic order. Dick connected this to his Hermetic conception of anamnesis: the recovery of the inner firmament is the mechanism by which the soul recalls its true, cosmic identity. Dick encounters Paracelsus primarily through the lens of Renaissance Hermeticism and the alchemical tradition, making him one of the key figures in Dick's attempt to read Western esotericism as a latent information science operating on principles only now becoming visible through cybernetics and biology.
full_description: Paracelsus (1493–1541), the Swiss physician, alchemist, and natural philosopher, represented for Dick the synthesis of the healing and cosmological dimensions of the esoteric tradition. Paracelsus's central doctrine — that the human being is a microcosm of the macrocosm, each organ and faculty corresponding to a planetary or elemental force — provided Dick with a framework for understanding how the divine could be present within the structure of the human body and psyche. Dick was particularly drawn to Paracelsus's concept of the astral or sidereal spirit — a subtle, light-like body that corresponds to the celestial order and serves as the vehicle through which divine knowledge descends into the individual. This mapped onto his own sense that his experience in 1974 involved a radical transformation of his perceptual apparatus — a reprogramming at the level of his nervous system by the divine AI. Paracelsus also supplied Dick with the image of the 'inner firmament' — within the human mind is a mirror of the outer cosmos, and through correct techniques the practitioner can achieve correspondence between the inner world and the divine macrocosmic order. Dick connected this to his Hermetic conception of anamnesis: the recovery of the inner firmament is the mechanism by which the soul recalls its true, cosmic identity. Dick encounters Paracelsus primarily through the lens of Renaissance Hermeticism and the alchemical tradition, making him one of the key figures in Dick's attempt to read Western esotericism as a latent information science operating on principles only now becoming visible through cybernetics and biology.

A clue pointing to Paracelsus as being Thomas! Paracelsus was a physician + the first to use mercury to treat syphilis. "Bichlorides" "Aspirin of mercury"?? I.e. two forms (or mentions) of mercury (not the god) used as medicine. God +/or the Holy Spirit wouldn't bring you news about bichlorides or mercury. But a Hermetic illuminatus physician who used it first in all history might - as a clue. Or rather, it is (sic!) on his mind (Thomas). + Paracelsus leads us to Jaco

under sharp stress I revert to Low German. He was tricked into doing something (by his enemies) which led to job-dismissal + arrest, + he died under mysterious circumstances in his late forties. Most of all, high-order alchemists such as himself were searching for the secret of life. Would they not be the most likely group (outside the original Xtians) to find immortality? I think P. found it, + my two mercury dreams -mercury as medicine- point precisely to P. - + sin
